title: Area of an L-shaped figure
problem: An L-shaped figure is formed by taking a $9 \text{ m} \times 7 \text{ m}$ rectangle and removing a $3 \text{ m} \times 4 \text{ m}$ rectangle from one corner. Find the area.

steps.instruction: Find the area of the large rectangle.
Find the area of the removed rectangle.
Subtract the removed area from the large rectangle.

title: Area of a shape with a triangle
problem: A composite shape consists of an $8 \text{ cm} \times 5 \text{ cm}$ rectangle with a triangle of base $8 \text{ cm}$ and height $3 \text{ cm}$ on top. Find the total area.

steps.instruction: Find the area of the rectangle using $A = l \times w$.
Find the area of the triangle using $A = \frac{1}{2} \times b \times h$.
Add the two areas to find the total.

title: Area of a shape with a semicircle
problem: A composite shape is formed by a $12 \text{ cm} \times 6 \text{ cm}$ rectangle with a semicircle of diameter $6 \text{ cm}$ attached to one of the shorter ends. Find the total area to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Find the area of the rectangle.
Find the radius of the semicircle. The diameter is $6 \text{ cm}$, so the radius is $r = 6 \div 2 = 3 \text{ cm}$.
Find the area of the semicircle using $A = \frac{1}{2} \pi r^2$.
Add the two areas to find the total.
